title: DST Solver Function — DST_Solver
text: Solves system using Discrete Sine Transform (DST-II) scaling.

title: DST Solver Function — DST_Solver
text: Dim Integer. dimension problem. Lambda numeric vector scaling factors. B sparse matrix used solver. U numeric vector representing initial state. Ns Integer. size segments processed DST.

title: DST Solver Function — DST_Solver
text: numeric vector normalized result.

title: DST Solver Function — DST_Solver
code: Lambda <- c(1, 2, 3, 4) result <- DST_Solver(1, Lambda, diag(Lambda), c(1, 2, 3, 4), 4) #> Error: Not an S4 object.

title: Simulate State and Observations — Simulate_State_Obser
text: Simulates state trajectory observations dynamical system.

title: Simulate State and Observations — Simulate_State_Obser
text: Dt Numeric. time step size. Ntau Integer. Number observation points. NtNtau Integer. Total number time steps. f user-defined function state dynamics. h user-defined function observations. Dim Integer. number dimensions. seed Nullable Integer. Optional random seed reproducibility.

title: Simulate State and Observations — Simulate_State_Obser
text: list state trajectory, observations, random seed used.

title: Simulate State and Observations — Simulate_State_Obser
code: f <- function(x) x * 0.1 h <- function(x) x + 0.5 result <- Simulate_State_Obser(0.01, 10, 100, f, h, 2, seed = 123) #> Random seed used: 123

what: Advanced Filtering Techniques for State Estimation — YauYauFilter-package
title: Advanced Filtering Techniques for State Estimation — YauYauFilter-package
text: Provides advanced filtering methods state estimation,     including discrete sine transform (DST) solvers, normalized exponential     functions, methods simulating state observations. package     supports deterministic stochastic systems state-space models.

title: Advanced Filtering Techniques for State Estimation — YauYauFilter-package
text: Yu Wang [aut, cre],     Xueda Wei [aut],     Yuhao Qi [aut],     Xinrui Luo [aut] Maintainer: Yu Wang <yourfault@somewhere.net>

title: Compute Matrix B — computeB
text: Computes matrix B using sparse Kronecker products user-defined functions.

title: Compute Matrix B — computeB
text: s numeric matrix grid points. D_list list sparse matrices differential operators. Dt Numeric. Time step size. Ds Numeric. Grid spacing. f user-defined function transformations. df derivative function. h function contributing diagonal updates.

title: Compute Matrix B — computeB
text: sparse matrix representing computed matrix B.

title: Compute Matrix B — computeB
code: D_list <- list(Diagonal(4), Diagonal(4)) #> Error in Diagonal(4): could not find function "Diagonal" result <- computeB(matrix(1:8, 4, 2), D_list, 0.01, 0.5, sin, cos, function(x) x^2) #> Error: object 'D_list' not found

title: Generate Derivative Function — generate_derivative
text: function generates numerical derivative function given vector-valued function. uses finite differences approximate derivative input function respect element input vector.

title: Generate Derivative Function — generate_derivative
text: f vector-valued function takes numeric vector x input returns numeric vector output. function f support evaluation point within domain interest.

title: Generate Derivative Function — generate_derivative
text: function computes numerical derivative input function f. returned function takes numeric vector x input returns: f outputs scalar, derivative numeric vector length x. f outputs vector, derivative Jacobian matrix (, j)-th entry represents partial derivative ith output respect jth input.

title: Generate Derivative Function — generate_derivative
text: function computes numerical derivatives using finite difference method. element input vector x, small perturbation (epsilon) applied approximate derivative. Specifically, derivative f point x approximated : $$\frac{\partial f_i}{\partial x_j} \approx \frac{f_i(x + \epsilon e_j) - f_i(x)}{\epsilon},$$ e_j unit vector direction jth component. method supports vector-valued functions computes partial derivatives components output respect input vector. result Jacobian matrix output multidimensional.

title: Generate Derivative Function — generate_derivative
code: # Define a vector-valued function f <- function(x) {   c(x[1]^2 + x[2], x[1] + sin(x[2])) }  # Generate the derivative function df <- generate_derivative(f)  # Test the derivative function x <- c(1, 2) df(x)  # Returns the numerical Jacobian of f at x #> [1]  2.0000000 -0.4161468

text: sparse matrix. B sparse matrix.

text: sparse matrix representing Kronecker product B.

title: Compute Kronecker Product of Sparse Matrices — kroneckerProduct
code: library(Matrix) A <- Diagonal(4) B <- Diagonal(4) result <- kroneckerProduct(A, B) #> Error in kroneckerProduct(A, B): could not find function "kroneckerProduct"

what: Function to Wrap and Update Output Iu with Progress Bar — wrap_outiu_function
title: Function to Wrap and Update Output Iu with Progress Bar — wrap_outiu_function
text: function computes updates matrix Iu based sequence input vectors, iterative updates, transformations. uses progress bar display computation progress performs differential equation solving DST_Solver function normalizing values using NormalizedExp function.

title: Function to Wrap and Update Output Iu with Progress Bar — wrap_outiu_function
code: wrap_outiu_function(s, NtNtau, Ntau, Nt, Dim, y, h, Lambda, B, Ns, NormalizedExp, DST_Solver)

title: Function to Wrap and Update Output Iu with Progress Bar — wrap_outiu_function
text: s numeric matrix size (number sequences x Dim) representing input state sequences. NtNtau Integer. total number time steps, including Ntau steps. Ntau Integer. number tau steps iterative process. Nt Integer. number regular time steps within tau interval. Dim Integer. dimension state space. y numeric matrix representing observed values time step. h user-defined function computes relationship s observations y. accept numeric vector return numeric vector. Lambda numeric vector parameters used within DST_Solver function. B numeric matrix used coefficient matrix DST_Solver function. Ns Integer. sample size scaling parameter used DST_Solver. NormalizedExp function normalizes exponential values input sequences. DST_Solver function solves differential equation given Dim, Lambda, B, inputs. return numeric vector length input.

title: Function to Wrap and Update Output Iu with Progress Bar — wrap_outiu_function
text: numeric matrix Iu size NtNtau x Dim, row corresponds time step column corresponds dimension state space. values represent updated state trajectory.

title: Function to Wrap and Update Output Iu with Progress Bar — wrap_outiu_function
text: function iteratively updates Iu matrix follows: Initializes state vector U using exponential function normalizes . Updates U time step applying DST_Solver observation function h. Computes weighted sums U s populate Iu matrix. Utilizes progress bar display computation progress user feedback. function ensures numerical stability normalizing U step handles multi-dimensional state spaces processing dimension independently.

title: Function to Wrap and Update Output Iu with Progress Bar — wrap_outiu_function
